title: MSc Thesis - KV Cache Compression via Low-Rank Tensor Decomposition
summary: Master's thesis at Universität Trier on compressing the runtime KV cache of LLMs as a 3rd-order tensor (n_h x T x d_h). Goes beyond prior 2D / weight-matrix approaches (PALU, TPA, xKV) by decomposing the cached activations themselves with Tucker, CP, TT and t-SVD. The production method is a partial Tucker decomposition plus a JL-rotated 4-bit residual, with per-layer rank allocation framed as constrained optimisation under a global memory budget M.
highlights: First systematic 3rd-order tensor-decomposition study of runtime KV cache activations on Mistral-7B-v0.3 (GQA) and LLaMA-2-13B (MHA), WikiText-2 calibration, A100 40GB. Tucker dominates CP / TT / t-SVD at every 2x-5x ratio.

2x compression at ~1.3% relative Frobenius error on K and 0.6-1.5% on V via Tucker plus a JL-rotated 4-bit residual, driven by a joint Lagrangian allocator - about 35-40x lower V-cache reconstruction error than xKV at the same ratio.

Free zone validated end-to-end: 2x-3x compression holds Mistral perplexity within ~0.1-0.4% of baseline (multi-seed), GSM8K 8-shot CoT accuracy within the Wilson 95% CI, and RULER NIAH retrieval at 100% (ctx 4096).
Identified a structural K/V asymmetry (V is 2-3x harder to compress) motivating a per-tensor decomposition, and showed K must be decomposed before RoPE and re-rotated at decode.
note: Thesis ongoing. With a reconstruction cache, compressed decode is ~3.7x slower than the DynamicCache baseline (down from a 75-100x naive projection); fused reconstruction-attention kernels are the next milestone.

title: Neuro-Symbolic Narrative Similarity (HeteroGNN + Text Fusion)
summary: Neuro-symbolic architecture for narrative similarity that fuses a structural graph channel with pretrained text embeddings via element-wise additive averaging. An LLM extracts narrative components (themes, course of action, outcome, plot type) into schema-validated JSON that populates a heterogeneous graph; a HeteroGNN produces a narrative-level embedding that is combined with a Gemini text embedding.
highlights: 72.5% accuracy on pairwise narrative similarity (best config); 95% precision on a 500-triple Tell-Me-Again cross-lingual subset across de, en, es, fr, it.
Trained on 1,900 synthetic story triplets with soft-margin triplet loss, then CORAL domain adaptation and recursive pseudo-labelling with high-confidence triplet mining.
Heterogeneous graph with 4 node types (narrative, action, theme, outcome); 3-layer HeteroConv + SAGEConv with per-node-type LayerNorm and residuals.

Interpretability: fused cosine decomposes additively as S_fused = S_GG + S_TT + S_cross with R^2 = 1.0; the cross term is empirically negligible (~0.0015 vs S_GG 0.223 and S_TT 0.133). Principal-angle analysis showed graph and text subspaces are near-orthogonal.

title: RAG Resume API
summary: A Retrieval-Augmented Generation system embedded in my portfolio site, letting visitors query my resume and personal documents in natural language. Built with LlamaIndex for indexing and vector search, Google Gemini 2.5 Flash for context-aware answers, and gemini-embedding-001 for embeddings. The FastAPI backend is deployed as a Dockerised container on Google Cloud Run (Free Tier, europe-west1).
highlights: Live AI assistant on the portfolio site for interactive Q&A about my background and projects.
RAG pipeline using LlamaIndex VectorStoreIndex with Google GenAI embeddings.
Context-aware answer generation via Google Gemini 2.5 Flash.
Fully containerised; deployed on Google Cloud Run with scale-to-zero for free-tier hosting.

title: Risk-Averse Optimization (CVaR across 3 Domains)
summary: Research case study at Universität Trier on risk-averse decision-making with Conditional Value at Risk (CVaR). Three case studies span static nonlinear optimisation, classification under perturbation, and closed-loop optimal control, with heavy-tailed (Student-t) uncertainty as a stress test.
highlights: Wobbly Rosenbrock: nominal vs CVaR optimisation under parameter uncertainty (CasADi + IPOPT). Tail-risk reduction is largest under heavy-tailed noise.
Linear SVM on perturbed Cats vs Dogs: CVaR-SVM (CVXPY + OSQP) improves mean accuracy 53% to 58% while shrinking the tail compared to nominal SVM.

Wine-fermentation MPC: closed-loop receding-horizon control over a 21-day simulation; CVaR scenario-based controller hits 0% constraint violations vs 100% for the nominal controller under severe model mismatch.

title: Reflective CoT Faithfulness Study (Think Twice)
summary: Co-built a reproducible evaluation framework testing whether reflective chain-of-thought (RCoT) prompting makes LLM reasoning more faithful to the model's actual decision process. Extends the counterfactual-simulatability approach of Turpin et al. (2023) to a three-stage reflective pipeline (initial CoT, critique, revision).
highlights: Factorial evaluation across 2 models x 3 prompting strategies (No-CoT, CoT, RCoT) x 3 bias types x 2 shot settings: 13 BIG-Bench Hard categories, 650 evaluation instances total.
RCoT reduced bias susceptibility by ~7 percentage points over standard CoT on average, across both models and all three bias types.
Quantified a model-level robustness gap: max bias rate 25.6% (Gemini) vs 69.2% (LLaMA); LLaMA CoT accuracy collapsed from 61.7% to 11.7% under the Answer-is-Always-A few-shot bias.

Qualitative finding ("doubling down on the lie"): the revision stage often ignores the critique and reinforces the original fabricated narrative. Structured reflection alone is not enough for faithful reasoning.

title: Amazon Electronics Recommender System
summary: End-to-end recommendation system on the Amazon Reviews 2023 Electronics dataset (~391k interactions, 41k users, 137k items). Implemented collaborative filtering, hybrid SBERT + metadata models, and a Two-Tower MLP; FAISS for candidate retrieval and an MLP re-ranker for production-grade serving.
highlights: Improved NDCG@10 from 0.305 (CF baseline) to 0.334 with the Two-Tower MLP - approximately 9.5% relative gain, validated with paired t-tests and Cohen's d over 5 seeds.
Increased cold-start hit rate by ~50% via SBERT text embeddings and hybrid features.
Two-stage serving pipeline (FAISS retrieval + MLP re-rank) with sub-100ms inference on CPU after warm start.
Deployed as a FastAPI REST API, containerised with Docker, hosted on Azure Container Apps (scale-to-zero). Bundled-checkpoint design cut cold start from 30-45s to 2-3s and memory from ~2GB to ~200MB.
note: Live demo may cold-start from scale-to-zero. The first request can take up to ~30s; subsequent requests respond quickly.

title: ML Kaggle Competitions (Top-3 in Three Challenges)
summary: Three Kaggle-style ML competitions inside a university Machine Learning course at Universität Trier. Top-3 finish in all three challenges.
highlights: Taxi Tip Prediction (Polars + Scikit-Learn): engineered temporal, behavioural and cost features; final model HistGradientBoostingClassifier with early stopping.

Image Classification (CNN from scratch in PyTorch): VGG-style CNN (3-32-64-128-256 channels) with BatchNorm, dropout and global average pooling; AdamW + cosine annealing + early stopping. No pretrained weights allowed.
LLM-based Classification: 4-bit quantised google/gemma-3-12b-it with few-shot retrieval of top-N similar training examples and categorical bucketisation for LLM-readable inputs.

title: Transformer-Based Sentiment Analysis (YouTube Comments)
summary: End-to-end sentiment classification for YouTube comments using a fine-tuned BERT model, served via a Flask REST API and containerised with Docker. CI/CD automates Docker image builds and pushes to Docker Hub.
highlights: Achieved F1 = 0.81 on an imbalanced dataset of 10,000+ YouTube comments.
Deployed an interactive Gradio demo on Hugging Face Spaces, plus an API endpoint for integrations.
Automated builds and publishing via GitHub Actions.

title: Glaucoma Detection from Retinal Fundus Images
summary: Detection of glaucoma from retinal fundus images using optic-disc/cup segmentation and SVM classification. Undergraduate final-year project, presented and published at ICCSP 2020 (IEEE).
highlights: Led a 4-member team and successfully published the work at ICCSP 2020.
Region-growing segmentation pipeline with SVM classification for automated screening.
